title: Contributing to rwevalidate
text: Thanks interest improving rwevalidate. Bug reports, feature ideas, pull requests welcome.

title: Contributing to rwevalidate
text: Please open issue https://github.com/tavakohr/rwevalidate/issues. bug, include R version, operating system, small reproducible example can.

title: Contributing to rwevalidate
text: need live PostgreSQL database develop test package. test suite runs synthetic OMOP CDM built -memory DuckDB database, R CMD check works offline. can also try package hand without database using bundled demo CDM:
code: # Install the development dependencies once install.packages(c("devtools", "duckdb"))  # Run the test suite (uses the DuckDB mock) devtools::test()  # Run the full package check devtools::check() con <- rwevalidate::example_cdm() rwevalidate::validate_cohort(   cdm_schema = "main", cohort_table = "cohort", cohort_id = 1,   con = con, vocab_schema = "main",   output_dir = tempfile("rwe_demo_"), render_html = FALSE ) rwevalidate::cdm_disconnect(con)

title: Contributing to rwevalidate
text: Fork repository create branch change. Keep change focused, add update tests tests/testthat/. change function’s documentation, regenerate help files devtools::document() (roxygen2::roxygenise()). Make sure devtools::check() passes new errors, warnings, notes. Open pull request describe change .

title: Contributing to rwevalidate
text: Every push main runs GitHub Actions : run R CMD check Ubuntu, macOS, Windows, build test-coverage report Codecov, build deploy pkgdown documentation site https://tavakohr.github.io/rwevalidate/. pull request runs R CMD check matrix, can see result merged.

title: Getting started with rwevalidate
text: pointing package real CDM, can see run end end small synthetic one. example_cdm() builds tiny OMOP CDM (10 patients, one heart-failure cohort) -memory DuckDB database returns live connection. chunk article actually runs, needs duckdb package. fixture entirely synthetic contains real patient data. Everything shows run call OMOP CDM.
code: library(rwevalidate)  con <- example_cdm()  out <- validate_cohort(   cdm_schema   = "main",   cohort_table = "cohort",   cohort_id    = 1,   con          = con,   vocab_schema = "main",   output_dir   = tempfile("rwe_demo_"),   render_html  = FALSE ) #> ℹ Running attrition audit (Module 2)... #> ℹ Running temporal data density (Module 3)... #> Module 1 (concept coverage) skipped; supply `concept_ids` to enable. #> Module 4 (covariate feasibility) skipped; supply `comparator_id` to enable. #> ✔ Validation complete: 0 fail, 0 warn across 2 checks.  out$report$check_summary #>                 section status                         maps_to #> 1      Cohort Attrition   pass HARPER Sec.5 / RECORD-PE Item 6 #> 2 Temporal Data Density   pass  FDA Reliability - data accrual #>               detail #> 1 All checks passed. #> 2 All checks passed. cdm_disconnect(con)

title: Getting started with rwevalidate
text: cdm_connect() opens PostgreSQL connection verifies required clinical tables exist cdm_schema returning. Many CDM builds split-schema: clinical tables one schema vocabulary (concept, concept_ancestor) another. Pass vocabulary schema via vocab_schema (default "vocab").
code: library(rwevalidate)  con <- cdm_connect(   host       = "localhost",   port       = 5432,   dbname     = "your_db",   user       = "your_user",   password   = "your_password",   cdm_schema = "mimic_cdm" )

title: Getting started with rwevalidate
text: rwevalidate reads cohort table columns subject_id, cohort_definition_id, cohort_start_date, cohort_end_date. example, heart-failure cohort using SNOMED concept 316139 descendants:
code: DBI::dbExecute(con, "   CREATE TABLE results.my_cohort AS   SELECT person_id AS subject_id, 1 AS cohort_definition_id,          MIN(condition_start_date) AS cohort_start_date,          MAX(condition_end_date)   AS cohort_end_date   FROM mimic_cdm.condition_occurrence   WHERE condition_concept_id IN (     SELECT descendant_concept_id FROM vocab.concept_ancestor     WHERE ancestor_concept_id = 316139)   GROUP BY person_id")

title: Getting started with rwevalidate
text: validate_cohort() runs modules writes report. Supply open con connection arguments directly. writes validation_report.html validation_results.json output_dir, returns list results (per-module output), report (paths + traffic-light summary), flags.
code: results <- validate_cohort(   cdm_schema    = "mimic_cdm",   cohort_table  = "results.my_cohort",   cohort_id     = 1,   concept_ids   = 316139,   # enables Module 1 (concept coverage)   comparator_id = 2,        # enables Module 4 (covariate feasibility)   vocab_schema  = "vocab",   output_dir    = "./validation_report",   con           = con ) results$report$check_summary   # one row per check: pass / warn / fail results$flags                  # all WARN:/FAIL: messages

title: Getting started with rwevalidate
text: module also callable need one view.
code: run_attrition(con, "mimic_cdm", "results.my_cohort", cohort_id = 1,               vocab_schema = "vocab")  run_density(con, "mimic_cdm", "results.my_cohort", cohort_id = 1)  run_concepts(con, "mimic_cdm", "results.my_cohort", cohort_id = 1,              concept_ids = 316139, vocab_schema = "vocab")  run_covariates(con, "mimic_cdm", "results.my_cohort", cohort_id = 1,                comparator_id = 2, vocab_schema = "vocab")

title: Getting started with rwevalidate
text: Every check returns "pass" (green), "warn" (amber), "fail" (red). Thresholds documented arguments – example prior-observation coverage warns 70% fails 50%, covariates |SMD| > 0.1 flagged imbalanced. Tune study’s requirements.
code: cdm_disconnect(con)

title: Validation: cohorts, datasets, and methodology
text: MIMIC-IV requires PhysioNet credentialing; MIMIC builds local. Eunomia public downloads demand via Eunomia package.

title: Validation: cohorts, datasets, and methodology
text: Cohorts spanning every index domain plus empty-cohort edge case, run validate_cohort() complement comparator. ran without error; empty cohort aborts clear message (intended guard).
code: kbl(data.frame(   cohort = c("Heart failure","Atrial fibrillation","Obesity","Acetaminophen",              "Oxycodone","Mech ventilation","CBC lymphocytes","Empty (edge)"),   domain = c("condition","condition","condition","drug","drug","procedure",              "measurement","condition"),   n = c(25,30,20,89,64,45,91,0),   status = c(rep("ok",7),"handled-error") ))

title: Validation: cohorts, datasets, and methodology
text: Five cohorts OHDSI Phenotype Library (via sibling omop-phenotype-pipeline project) instantiated mimiciv_omop. four modules ran cohorts 481-849 patients.
code: kbl(data.frame(   phenotype = c("Type 2 diabetes","Cardiac valve + AF","Acute liver injury",                 "Drug-induced pancreatitis","Treatment-resistant depression"),   cohort_id = c(288, 1103, 736, 253, 1009),   n_target = c(645, 481, 849, 485, 775),   status = "ok (4 checks)" ))

title: Validation: cohorts, datasets, and methodology
text: tool Eunomia/GiBleed returns data-appropriate traffic light: attrition passes (full longitudinal histories) MIMIC fails (ICU-short windows), power adequate large N. Evidence checks reflect real data characteristics rather fixed output.

title: Validation: cohorts, datasets, and methodology
text: harness scripts live data-raw/ source repository (run_battery.R, run_phenotypes.R, run_eunomia.R). part installed package. steps method.

title: Validation: cohorts, datasets, and methodology
text: Production CDMs usually keep vocabulary separate schema. cdm_connect() validates clinical tables cdm_schema vocabulary tables vocab_schema.
code: con <- cdm_connect(   dbname = "FHIR", user = "...", password = "...",   cdm_schema = "mimiciv_omop",   # clinical   vocab_schema = "vocab"          # concept, concept_ancestor )

title: Validation: cohorts, datasets, and methodology
text: mimiciv_omop build ships without observation_period. Derive one row per person spanning first last dated event across clinical domains:
code: DBI::dbExecute(con, "   CREATE TABLE mimiciv_omop.observation_period AS   SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY person_id) AS observation_period_id,          person_id,          MIN(d) AS observation_period_start_date,          MAX(d) AS observation_period_end_date,          44814724 AS period_type_concept_id   FROM (     SELECT person_id, condition_start_date d FROM mimiciv_omop.condition_occurrence     UNION ALL SELECT person_id, drug_exposure_start_date FROM mimiciv_omop.drug_exposure     UNION ALL SELECT person_id, measurement_date FROM mimiciv_omop.measurement     UNION ALL SELECT person_id, procedure_date FROM mimiciv_omop.procedure_occurrence     UNION ALL SELECT person_id, visit_start_date FROM mimiciv_omop.visit_occurrence   ) e WHERE d IS NOT NULL GROUP BY person_id")

title: Validation: cohorts, datasets, and methodology
text: phenotype, expand gold seed concepts descendants via concept_ancestor, remove excluded descendants, index first qualifying event across domains. subject_id NUMERIC mimiciv_omop person ids exceed bigint.
code: DBI::dbExecute(con, "   INSERT INTO results.phenotype_cohorts   SELECT person_id, 288 AS cohort_definition_id, MIN(d), MAX(d) FROM (     SELECT person_id, condition_start_date d FROM mimiciv_omop.condition_occurrence      WHERE condition_concept_id IN (        SELECT descendant_concept_id FROM vocab.concept_ancestor         WHERE ancestor_concept_id IN ( /* gold seeds */ )           AND descendant_concept_id NOT IN (             SELECT descendant_concept_id FROM vocab.concept_ancestor              WHERE ancestor_concept_id IN ( /* excluded */ )))     /* UNION ALL the drug/measurement/procedure domains likewise */   ) e GROUP BY person_id")

title: Validation: cohorts, datasets, and methodology
text: exact seed excluded concept ids five phenotypes ship package can read back: authoritative cohort definitions OHDSI Phenotype Library entries cohort ids 288, 1103, 736, 253, 1009 (https://github.com/OHDSI/PhenotypeLibrary). numeric seed ids recorded , reproducibility; definitions copied.
code: seeds <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "phenotype_seeds.csv",                               package = "rwevalidate")) kbl(seeds[, c("cohort_id", "phenotype", "n_seed", "n_excluded")]) # Example: the Type 2 diabetes seed concept ids strsplit(seeds$seed_concept_ids[seeds$phenotype == "t2dm"], ";")[[1]] #>  [1] "443238"   "201820"   "442793"   "195771"   "37392407" "4184637"  #>  [7] "40484649" "42689695" "765533"   "43531006" "765650"   "45770986" #> [13] "201254"   "45768456" "40484648" "4128019"  "435216"

title: Validation: cohorts, datasets, and methodology
code: validate_cohort(   cdm_schema   = "mimiciv_omop",   cohort_table = "results.phenotype_cohorts",   cohort_id    = 288,   comparator_id = 10288,           # complement   concept_ids  = c(443238, 201820, 442793),  # seeds -> Module 1   concept_domain = "condition",   vocab_schema = "vocab",   output_dir   = "./validation_report" )

title: Validation: cohorts, datasets, and methodology
text: Summary tables ship inst/extdata/:
code: list.files(system.file("extdata", package = "rwevalidate")) #> [1] "phenotype_seeds.csv"    "phenotype_summary.csv"  "robustness_summary.csv"

what: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: rwevalidate R package validates patient cohorts real-world evidence (RWE) studies OMOP Common Data Model data. single function call runs four validation modules, produces self-contained HTML report machine-readable JSON sidecar aligned FDA guidance HARPER protocol. package built existing OHDSI tool combines cohort-level attrition auditing, temporal data density, concept coverage, covariate balance one regulator-ready output. depend CohortDiagnostics, DataQualityDashboard, Achilles, installs runs R environment DBI connection OMOP CDM. Current status: v0.1 complete live-tested MIMIC-IV PostgreSQL. four modules available. package website tavakohr.github.io/rwevalidate.

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: submit RWE study regulator, always ask two questions. First, data relevant research question? words, right patients captured, right concepts? Second, data reliable enough support causal inference? words, follow-complete, records frequent enough time, two cohorts comparable? Today, answering questions clear reproducible way means combining several tools writing custom SQL hand. rwevalidate work . give cohort table already exists database, returns one list object formatted report. Every check report maps directly regulatory framework.

what: The Regulatory Framework Behind Each Validation
title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: Every check rwevalidate traces back published regulatory document reporting standard. table shows module maps framework .

previous_headings: The Regulatory Framework Behind Each Validation
title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: FDA RWE Guidance (2023) U.S. Food Drug Administration. Considerations Use Real-World Data Real-World Evidence Support Regulatory Decision-Making Drug Biological Products. Guidance Industry. Silver Spring, MD: FDA; 2023. Available : www.fda.gov/media/171667/download FDA guidance looks RWE studies two angles. Relevance asks whether data source captures population, exposure, outcome, covariates study wants measure. Reliability asks whether data complete, recorded consistent way, free systematic dropout bias outcomes captured. Modules 1 3 rwevalidate answer two questions directly. HARPER Protocol (2022) Wang SV, Pottegard , Crown W, et al. HARmonized Protocol Template Enhance Reproducibility hypothesis evaluating real-world evidence studies treatment effects: good practices report joint ISPE/ISPOR task force. Pharmacoepidemiol Drug Saf. 2023;32(1):44-55. Available : doi.org/10.1002/pds.5507 HARPER structured protocol template designed make pharmacoepidemiology RWE studies reproducible independently verifiable data analysis begins. Section 4 specifies covariates defined balanced across arms. Section 5 specifies cohort definition criteria sequential attrition documented. Modules 2 4 produce output can populate two sections directly. RECORD-PE (2015, extended 2018) Benchimol EI, Smeeth L, Guttmann , et al. REporting studies Conducted using Observational Routinely-collected health Data (RECORD) Statement. PLoS Med. 2015;12(10):e1001885. Langan SM, Schmidt SA, Wing K, et al. reporting studies conducted using observational routinely collected health data statement pharmacoepidemiology (RECORD-PE). BMJ. 2018;363:k3532. Available : doi.org/10.1136/bmj.k3532 Item 6 RECORD-PE checklist requires patient flowchart showing many individuals identified stage cohort construction excluded. run_attrition() reports cohort-level counts item asks (size, index dates, prior observation, demographics). yet rebuild step--step flowchart , instantiated cohort table carry criteria produced . Per-criterion attrition planned later version.

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: validate_cohort() main entry point. connects CDM, runs module sequence, collects flags, calls render_validation_report() produce final outputs. connection management handled internally: pass open con left open function exits; pass PostgreSQL credentials connection opened closed automatically. four modules independent . one queries CDM directly using DBI::dbGetQuery() parameterized glue templates, returns named list, appends threshold violations WARN: FAIL: strings flags vector. orchestrator validate_cohort() collects flags feeds traffic-light summary report.
code: validate_cohort()     |     +-- cdm_connect()              # opens DBI connection, validates tables exist     |     +-- run_concepts()  [opt]      # Module 1: concept coverage (needs concept_ids)     +-- run_attrition()            # Module 2: attrition audit (always runs)     +-- run_density()              # Module 3: temporal data density (always runs)     +-- run_covariates() [opt]     # Module 4: covariate balance (needs comparator_id)     |     +-- render_validation_report() # builds HTML + JSON from module outputs     |     +-- cdm_disconnect()           # closes connection if opened internally

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: rwevalidate handles CDM environments clinical vocabulary tables live separate schemas. cdm_schema argument points tables like condition_occurrence, person, observation_period. vocab_schema argument points concept concept_ancestor. matters CDM deployments (including MIMIC-IV OMOP build) populate vocabulary schema separately, leaving clinical schema’s concept table empty. vocabulary lookups package route vocab_schema.

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: thresholds function arguments documented defaults. Nothing hardcoded.

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: Every run writes two files output_dir: validation_report.html: self-contained HTML file embedded plots, tables, traffic-light summary. server needed open . validation_results.json: machine-readable sidecar numeric results, flags, run metadata, package version. Useful downstream pipelines audit logs. HTML report rendered R Markdown template using rmarkdown::render(). Pandoc available, validate_cohort() falls back writing JSON sidecar emits warning rather error.

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: Requires R >= 4.1. HTML rendering requires Pandoc, bundled RStudio. running command line without RStudio, set environment variable RSTUDIO_PANDOC folder containing Pandoc binary.
code: # Install from GitHub remotes::install_github("tavakohr/rwevalidate")  # Or from a local clone install.packages(".", repos = NULL, type = "source") # Example for command-line / Rscript users Sys.setenv(RSTUDIO_PANDOC = "/usr/lib/rstudio/resources/app/bin/quarto/bin/tools")

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: need live database see package work. example_cdm() builds tiny synthetic OMOP CDM -memory DuckDB database returns live connection can pass straight validate_cohort(). needs duckdb package installed (install.packages("duckdb")).
code: library(rwevalidate)  con <- example_cdm()   # tiny synthetic OMOP CDM in in-memory DuckDB  out <- validate_cohort(   cdm_schema   = "main",       # the demo CDM uses the default DuckDB schema   cohort_table = "cohort",   cohort_id    = 1,   con          = con,   vocab_schema = "main",   output_dir   = tempfile("rwe_demo_"),   render_html  = FALSE         # write the JSON sidecar only, no Pandoc needed )  out$report$check_summary       # traffic-light table: attrition + density both pass cdm_disconnect(con)

what: What is in the demo CDM
title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: fixture entirely synthetic. contains MIMIC-IV real patient data, safe ship runs offline. holds 10 fake patients one heart-failure cohort, every table attrition density modules query: deliberately omits comparator arm rich vocabulary, exercises default attrition density checks Module 1 (concept coverage) Module 4 (covariate feasibility). , real analysis, point package actual OMOP CDM shown .

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
code: library(rwevalidate)  out <- validate_cohort(   cdm_schema     = "mimic_cdm",   cohort_table   = "results.rwevalidate_test_cohort",   cohort_id      = 1,   vocab_schema   = "vocab",   obs_window     = c(-365, 0),      # 365 days prior observation required   density_window = c(-365, 365),    # 1 year before and after index   output_dir     = "./validation_report",   host           = "localhost",   port           = 5432,   dbname         = "FHIR",   user           = "your_user",   password       = "your_password" )  # Review collected flags out$flags  # Review traffic-light summary table out$report$check_summary

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
code: out <- validate_cohort(   cdm_schema     = "mimic_cdm",   cohort_table   = "results.rwevalidate_test_cohort",   cohort_id      = 1,   comparator_id  = 2,               # enables Module 4 (covariate balance)   concept_ids    = c(316139),       # enables Module 1 (concept coverage)   concept_domain = "condition",   vocab_schema   = "vocab",   obs_window     = c(-365, 0),   density_window = c(-365, 365),   output_dir     = "./validation_report",   host = "localhost", port = 5432,   dbname = "FHIR", user = "your_user", password = "your_password" )

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
code: con <- cdm_connect(   host = "localhost", port = 5432,   dbname = "FHIR", user = "your_user", password = "your_password",   cdm_schema = "mimic_cdm", vocab_schema = "vocab" )  out <- validate_cohort(   con            = con,       # connection is left open when validate_cohort() exits   cdm_schema     = "mimic_cdm",   cohort_table   = "results.rwevalidate_test_cohort",   cohort_id      = 1,   output_dir     = "./validation_report" )  DBI::dbDisconnect(con)

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: module can called need part validation.
code: con <- cdm_connect(...)  # Module 2 only: attrition audit attrition <- run_attrition(   con,   cdm_schema   = "mimic_cdm",   cohort_table = "results.rwevalidate_test_cohort",   cohort_id    = 1,   obs_window   = c(-365, 0) )  attrition$cohort_size       # integer: distinct subjects attrition$obs_coverage      # data.frame: coverage statistics attrition$demographics      # data.frame: age and sex at index attrition$flags             # character vector: any WARN:/FAIL: messages

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: Maps FDA RWE Guidance: Relevance. Checks whether concepts used define cohort actually present CDM, well mapped standard vocabulary, proportion cohort recorded event concept descendants. expands seed concept IDs concept_ancestor table computes prevalence mapping rates per domain.
code: run_concepts(   con, cdm_schema, cohort_table, cohort_id,   concept_ids    = c(316139),    # seed SNOMED concept ID for heart failure   domain         = "condition",   vocab_schema   = "vocab",   prev_warn_pct  = 10,           # warn if prevalence below 10%   prev_fail_pct  = 1             # fail if prevalence below 1% )

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: Maps HARPER Protocol Section 5 RECORD-PE Item 6. Profiles cohort population four queries: (2a) total distinct subjects, (2b) index date distribution year, (2c) prior observation coverage relative required lookback window, (2d) age sex index date. Flags raised prior observation coverage falls warn fail threshold.
code: run_attrition(   con, cdm_schema, cohort_table, cohort_id,   obs_window    = c(-365, 0),   vocab_schema  = "vocab",   obs_warn_pct  = 70,   obs_fail_pct  = 50 )

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: Maps FDA RWE Guidance: Reliability. Computes records per patient per month relative index date across four clinical domains: conditions, drugs, measurements, visits. Also produces follow-completeness table showing subject’s observation period ends (death, cohort exit, end available data). Sparse density specific months can indicate data gaps coding practice changes.
code: run_density(   con, cdm_schema, cohort_table, cohort_id,   obs_window = c(-365, 365) )

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: Maps HARPER Protocol Section 4. Requires comparator cohort. Computes standardized mean differences (SMDs) binary continuous covariates across target comparator arms, ranks top concepts prevalence, estimates study power. SMD values 0.1 conventionally considered imbalanced flagged.
code: run_covariates(   con, cdm_schema, cohort_table, cohort_id,   comparator_id = 2,   vocab_schema  = "vocab",   obs_window    = c(-365, 0) )

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: HTML report six sections. section corresponds one regulatory framework requirement. Section 2 populated concept_ids supplied. Section 5 populated comparator_id supplied. show placeholder text otherwise report structure stays consistent across runs.

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: CDM version: OMOP CDM v5.3.1 (v5.4 episode tables used). validate_cohort() warns connected CDM reports version outside 5.3.x. Databases: PostgreSQL (primary). DuckDB supported testing via testthat mock. SQL dialect: Queries written PostgreSQL DuckDB SQL run directly DBI. yet translated SqlRender, OHDSI backends (SQL Server, Redshift, Oracle, BigQuery) supported version. Multi-dialect support via SqlRender roadmap OHDSI network use. Cohort input: Instantiated cohort tables . table must subject_id, cohort_definition_id, cohort_start_date, cohort_end_date columns. ATLAS JSON parsing supported. OHDSI tool chain required: package imports lightweight CRAN packages (DBI, RPostgres, jsonlite, rmarkdown, glue, cli, utils; ggplot2 soft dependency used report plots) require CohortDiagnostics, DataQualityDashboard, Achilles, DatabaseConnector.

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: Bug reports, feature ideas, pull requests welcome. Development testing run offline synthetic DuckDB CDM, need live database. See CONTRIBUTING.md setup, testing, pull-request process.

title: Validate Patient Cohorts for Real-World Evidence Studies on OMOP CDM
text: MIT. See LICENSE.

what: Build the traffic-light validation summary table — build_check_summary
title: Build the traffic-light validation summary table — build_check_summary
text: Collects module's flags one row per validation section, mapping regulatory framework section addresses.

title: Build the traffic-light validation summary table — build_check_summary
code: build_check_summary(results)

title: Build the traffic-light validation summary table — build_check_summary
text: results combined results list (named module outputs, e.g. attrition, density). Modules absent list skipped.

title: Build the traffic-light validation summary table — build_check_summary
text: data.frame columns section, status, maps_to, detail.

what: Connect to an OMOP CDM database — cdm_connect
title: Connect to an OMOP CDM database — cdm_connect
text: Opens PostgreSQL connection via RPostgres validates required clinical tables (person, observation_period, condition_occurrence, drug_exposure, measurement, visit_occurrence, death) exist cdm_schema vocabulary tables (concept, concept_ancestor) exist vocab_schema returning. split-schema CDMs (clinical vocabulary different schemas) pass vocab_schema; defaults cdm_schema single-schema builds.

title: Connect to an OMOP CDM database — cdm_connect
code: cdm_connect(   host = "localhost",   port = 5432,   dbname,   user,   password,   cdm_schema,   vocab_schema = cdm_schema )

title: Connect to an OMOP CDM database — cdm_connect
text: host Database host. Default "localhost". port Database port. Default 5432. dbname Database name. user Database user. password Database password. cdm_schema Schema holding clinical CDM tables. vocab_schema Schema holding vocabulary tables. Defaults cdm_schema.

title: Connect to an OMOP CDM database — cdm_connect
text: live DBI connection CDM database.

title: Connect to an OMOP CDM database — cdm_connect
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- cdm_connect(   dbname = "omop", user = "me", password = "secret",   cdm_schema = "mimiciv_omop", vocab_schema = "vocab" ) cdm_disconnect(con) } # }

what: Disconnect from a CDM database — cdm_disconnect
title: Disconnect from a CDM database — cdm_disconnect
text: Disconnect CDM database

title: Disconnect from a CDM database — cdm_disconnect
code: cdm_disconnect(con)

title: Disconnect from a CDM database — cdm_disconnect
text: con live DBI connection returned cdm_connect().

title: Disconnect from a CDM database — cdm_disconnect
text: Invisibly returns result DBI::dbDisconnect().

what: Classify a vector of module flags into a traffic-light status — classify_status
title: Classify a vector of module flags into a traffic-light status — classify_status
text: Flags strings prefixed "FAIL:" "WARN:" (see module functions). FAIL -> "fail" (red); else WARN -> "warn" (amber); else "pass" (green).

title: Classify a vector of module flags into a traffic-light status — classify_status
code: classify_status(flags)

title: Classify a vector of module flags into a traffic-light status — classify_status
text: flags Character vector flag messages (may empty).

title: Classify a vector of module flags into a traffic-light status — classify_status
text: single string: "pass", "warn", "fail".

what: Create a small in-memory OMOP CDM for examples and demos — example_cdm
title: Create a small in-memory OMOP CDM for examples and demos — example_cdm
text: Builds tiny synthetic OMOP CDM (10 persons) -memory DuckDB database returns live connection. Every table rwevalidate queries attrition density modules populated, together cohort table standard subject_id, cohort_definition_id, cohort_start_date, cohort_end_date columns. clinical tables minimal vocabulary live default main schema.

title: Create a small in-memory OMOP CDM for examples and demos — example_cdm
code: example_cdm()

title: Create a small in-memory OMOP CDM for examples and demos — example_cdm
text: live DBI connection -memory DuckDB OMOP CDM.

title: Create a small in-memory OMOP CDM for examples and demos — example_cdm
text: exists package can tried, examples can run, without live database connection. synthetic demo fixture, substitute real CDM. duckdb package (soft dependency) must installed. caller owns returned connection close cdm_disconnect().

title: Create a small in-memory OMOP CDM for examples and demos — example_cdm
code: if (requireNamespace("duckdb", quietly = TRUE)) {   con <- example_cdm()   attrition <- run_attrition(con, cdm_schema = "main",                              cohort_table = "cohort", cohort_id = 1,                              vocab_schema = "main")   print(attrition$cohort_size)   cdm_disconnect(con) } #> duckdb: caching downloaded extensions in the package library: #> ℹ /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/duckdb/extensions #> ℹ This is removed when the package is re-installed; see `?duckdb_storage` to choose a different location. #> [1] 10

what: Render the validation report (HTML) and write the JSON sidecar — render_validation_report
title: Render the validation report (HTML) and write the JSON sidecar — render_validation_report
text: Renders bundled R Markdown template self-contained HTML report writes machine-readable validation_results.json alongside .

title: Render the validation report (HTML) and write the JSON sidecar — render_validation_report
code: render_validation_report(   results,   output_dir,   cdm_schema,   cohort_id,   package_version = as.character(utils::packageVersion("rwevalidate")),   run_date = Sys.time(),   quiet = TRUE )

title: Render the validation report (HTML) and write the JSON sidecar — render_validation_report
text: results Combined results list (e.g. list(attrition = ..., density = ...), optionally data_source = ...). output_dir Directory write report (created needed). cdm_schema CDM schema name (report header / JSON). cohort_id Cohort definition id (report header / JSON). package_version Package version string. Defaults installed rwevalidate version. run_date Timestamp run. Defaults base::Sys.time(). quiet Passed rmarkdown::render(). Default TRUE.

title: Render the validation report (HTML) and write the JSON sidecar — render_validation_report
text: Invisibly, list html, json, check_summary.

title: Module 2 - Cohort Attrition Audit — run_attrition
text: Profiles instantiated cohort: size, index-date distribution, prior observation coverage, demographics index. Maps HARPER section 5 RECORD-PE item 6. thresholds arguments.

title: Module 2 - Cohort Attrition Audit — run_attrition
code: run_attrition(   con,   cdm_schema,   cohort_table,   cohort_id,   obs_window = c(-365, 0),   vocab_schema = "vocab",   obs_warn_pct = 70,   obs_fail_pct = 50,   attrition_warn_threshold = 0.5 )

title: Module 2 - Cohort Attrition Audit — run_attrition
text: con live DBI connection (see cdm_connect()). cdm_schema Schema holding clinical CDM tables (e.g. "mimic_cdm"). cohort_table Cohort table, schema-qualified (e.g. "results.rwevalidate_test_cohort"). Must columns subject_id, cohort_definition_id, cohort_start_date, cohort_end_date. cohort_id Integer cohort definition id profile. obs_window Length-2 numeric c(pre, post) days relative index. required prior-observation window abs(obs_window[1]) days (default 365). vocab_schema Schema holding vocabulary tables (concept). split-schema environment clinical schema's concept table empty, gender label resolved vocab_schema. Default "vocab". obs_warn_pct, obs_fail_pct Prior-observation coverage thresholds (percent cohort sufficient prior observation). obs_warn_pct flags WARN, obs_fail_pct flags FAIL. Defaults 70 50. attrition_warn_threshold Reserved stepwise inclusion-criteria attrition (single-criterion drop fraction). exercised v0.1 criteria lists yet supported input. Default 0.5.

title: Module 2 - Cohort Attrition Audit — run_attrition
text: named list: cohort_size integer - distinct subjects cohort index_date_summary data.frame index_year, n obs_coverage data.frame: cohort_n, n_sufficient, pct_sufficient, min_prior_days. cohort_n counts every cohort subject. subject whose index date falls outside observation period counts insufficient prior observation rather dropped, cohort_n matches cohort_size. demographics data.frame: sex, mean_age, min_age, max_age, n flags character vector WARN:/FAIL: messages (empty pass)

title: Module 2 - Cohort Attrition Audit — run_attrition
text: profiles cohort handed . reports cohort-level counts item 6 asks , yet rebuild step--step attrition flowchart (many subjects inclusion exclusion criterion removed), cohort table carry criteria built . Per-criterion attrition planned later version.

title: Module 1 - Concept Coverage — run_concepts
text: Profiles whether cohort-defining concepts present, well-mapped, richly represented CDM. Maps FDA RWE Relevance (data fit question?). first module read vocabulary schema.

title: Module 1 - Concept Coverage — run_concepts
code: run_concepts(   con,   cdm_schema,   cohort_table = NULL,   cohort_id = NULL,   concept_ids,   domain = "condition",   vocab_schema = "vocab",   prevalence_warn_pct = 10,   prevalence_fail_pct = 1,   unmapped_warn_pct = 20 )

title: Module 1 - Concept Coverage — run_concepts
text: con live DBI connection (see cdm_connect()). cdm_schema Schema holding clinical CDM tables. cohort_table Cohort table, schema-qualified. Optional currently unused; reserved cohort-restricted prevalence later version. cohort_id Integer cohort definition id. Optional reserved, see cohort_table. concept_ids Numeric vector cohort-defining seed concept id(s). descendants expanded via {vocab_schema}.concept_ancestor. domain Domain seed concepts live ; one names internal domain map ("condition", "drug", "measurement", "procedure"). Default "condition". vocab_schema Schema holding vocabulary tables. Default "vocab". prevalence_warn_pct, prevalence_fail_pct Concept-prevalence thresholds (percent CDM persons least one descendant record). warn flags WARN, fail flags FAIL. Defaults 10 1. unmapped_warn_pct Per-domain unmapped-record threshold (percent mapped concept_id = 0) WARN flagged. Default 20.

title: Module 1 - Concept Coverage — run_concepts
text: named list: prevalence data.frame: n_with, n_total, pct ancestor_coverage data.frame: total_descendants, present_descendants, pct_present mapping_by_domain data.frame: domain, n_records, n_unmapped, pct_mapped flags character vector WARN:/FAIL: messages

title: Module 4 - Covariate Feasibility — run_covariates
text: Compares target cohort comparator baseline covariates: standardized mean differences (SMD love-plot data), per-arm prevalence table, simple power calculation. Maps HARPER Sec.4 (comparability). Runs comparator_id supplied.

title: Module 4 - Covariate Feasibility — run_covariates
code: run_covariates(   con,   cdm_schema,   cohort_table,   cohort_id,   comparator_id,   vocab_schema = "vocab",   covariate_window = c(-365, 0),   top_n = 20,   smd_warn_threshold = 0.1,   power_baseline_rate = 0.15,   power_effect_rate = 0.3,   power_sig_level = 0.05,   power_warn_threshold = 0.8 )

title: Module 4 - Covariate Feasibility — run_covariates
text: con live DBI connection (see cdm_connect()). cdm_schema Schema holding clinical CDM tables. cohort_table Cohort table, schema-qualified, containing arms. cohort_id Integer cohort definition id target arm. comparator_id Integer cohort definition id comparator arm. vocab_schema Schema holding vocabulary tables. Default "vocab". covariate_window Length-2 numeric c(pre, post) days defining baseline window relative index comorbidity/drug covariates. Default c(-365, 0). top_n Number top conditions/drugs profile. Default 20. smd_warn_threshold Absolute SMD covariate flagged imbalanced. Default 0.1 (standard). power_baseline_rate, power_effect_rate Assumed comparator target event rates two-proportion power calculation. Defaults 0.15, 0.30. power_sig_level Significance level power calculation. Default 0.05. power_warn_threshold Power value flags WARN. Default 0.80.

title: Module 4 - Covariate Feasibility — run_covariates
text: named list: smd_table, prevalence_table, power, flags.

title: Module 3 - Temporal Data Density — run_density
text: Profiles longitudinal data accrual around index date: record density per patient per month across clinical domains, follow-completeness censoring-reason breakdown. Maps FDA RWE Reliability (data accrual).

title: Module 3 - Temporal Data Density — run_density
code: run_density(   con,   cdm_schema,   cohort_table,   cohort_id,   obs_window = c(-365, 365),   sparse_warn_min = 1 )

title: Module 3 - Temporal Data Density — run_density
text: con live DBI connection (see cdm_connect()). cdm_schema Schema holding clinical CDM tables (e.g. "mimic_cdm"). cohort_table Cohort table, schema-qualified. cohort_id Integer cohort definition id. obs_window Length-2 numeric c(pre, post) days relative index. Density computed records inside [index + pre, index + post]. Default c(-365, 365). sparse_warn_min Minimum total records domain must within window considered present; domains flag WARN (likely missing/sparse domain). Default 1 (flag truly empty domains).

title: Module 3 - Temporal Data Density — run_density
text: named list: density_by_domain data.frame: domain, months_from_index, n_patients, n_records, records_per_patient followup_summary data.frame: one row per censoring_reason n median_followup_days (median group) followup_detail per-subject data.frame: subject_id, effective_end_date, followup_days, censoring_reason flags character vector WARN:/FAIL: messages

what: Validate a patient cohort for real-world-evidence use — validate_cohort
title: Validate a patient cohort for real-world-evidence use — validate_cohort
text: One-call entry point: connects OMOP CDM (uses supplied connection), runs attrition (Module 2) temporal-density (Module 3) checks, produces self-contained HTML report plus JSON sidecar aligned FDA RWE HARPER frameworks.

title: Validate a patient cohort for real-world-evidence use — validate_cohort
code: validate_cohort(   cdm_schema,   cohort_table,   cohort_id = 1,   con = NULL,   vocab_schema = "vocab",   concept_ids = NULL,   concept_domain = "condition",   comparator_id = NULL,   obs_window = c(-365, 0),   density_window = c(-365, 365),   output_dir = "./validation_report",   host = "localhost",   port = 5432,   dbname = NULL,   user = NULL,   password = NULL,   render_html = TRUE,   quiet = TRUE )

title: Validate a patient cohort for real-world-evidence use — validate_cohort
text: cdm_schema Schema holding clinical CDM tables (e.g. "mimic_cdm"). cohort_table Cohort table, schema-qualified (e.g. "results.rwevalidate_test_cohort"). cohort_id Integer cohort definition id. Default 1. con Optional open DBI connection. NULL (default) one opened connection arguments closed exit. vocab_schema Schema holding vocabulary tables. Default "vocab". Required clinical schema's concept table may empty split-schema builds. concept_ids Optional numeric vector cohort-defining seed concept id(s). supplied, Module 1 (concept coverage) runs populates report Section 2. NULL (default) Module 1 skipped. concept_domain Domain seed concepts live ("condition", "drug", "measurement", "procedure"). Default "condition". comparator_id Optional integer cohort definition id comparator arm. supplied, Module 4 (covariate feasibility) runs populates report Section 5. NULL (default) Module 4 skipped. obs_window Length-2 numeric c(pre, post) days attrition prior observation. Default c(-365, 0). density_window Length-2 numeric c(pre, post) days density. Default c(-365, 365). output_dir Directory report + JSON. Default "./validation_report". host, port, dbname, user, password PostgreSQL connection arguments, used con NULL. render_html Render HTML report. Default TRUE. Pandoc unavailable, JSON sidecar written (warning). quiet Passed rmarkdown::render(). Default TRUE.

title: Validate a patient cohort for real-world-evidence use — validate_cohort
text: Invisibly, list results (module outputs + data_source), report (paths + check summary), flags (collected flags).

title: Validate a patient cohort for real-world-evidence use — validate_cohort
text: Supply either open con (e.g. DuckDB testing) PostgreSQL connection arguments (dbname, user, password, ...). Connections opened internally closed exit; connection passed via con left open.

title: Validate a patient cohort for real-world-evidence use — validate_cohort
code: # Runnable demo on a small in-memory DuckDB CDM (no database needed). # render_html = FALSE writes only the JSON sidecar, so Pandoc is not required. if (requireNamespace("duckdb", quietly = TRUE)) {   con <- example_cdm()   out <- validate_cohort(     cdm_schema   = "main",     cohort_table = "cohort",     cohort_id    = 1,     con          = con,     vocab_schema = "main",     output_dir   = tempfile("rwe_demo_"),     render_html  = FALSE   )   print(out$report$check_summary)   cdm_disconnect(con) } #> ℹ Running attrition audit (Module 2)... #> ℹ Running temporal data density (Module 3)... #> Module 1 (concept coverage) skipped; supply `concept_ids` to enable. #> Module 4 (covariate feasibility) skipped; supply `comparator_id` to enable. #> ✔ Validation complete: 0 fail, 0 warn across 2 checks. #>                 section status                         maps_to #> 1      Cohort Attrition   pass HARPER Sec.5 / RECORD-PE Item 6 #> 2 Temporal Data Density   pass  FDA Reliability - data accrual #>               detail #> 1 All checks passed. #> 2 All checks passed.  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Against a live PostgreSQL OMOP CDM: validate_cohort(   cdm_schema   = "mimic_cdm",   cohort_table = "results.rwevalidate_test_cohort",   cohort_id    = 1,   dbname = "FHIR", user = "me", password = "secret",   vocab_schema = "vocab",   output_dir   = "./validation_report" ) } # }

title: Write the JSON sidecar (no rendering) — write_results_json

title: Write the JSON sidecar (no rendering) — write_results_json
code: write_results_json(   results,   output_dir,   cdm_schema,   cohort_id,   package_version = as.character(utils::packageVersion("rwevalidate")),   run_date = Sys.time(),   check_summary = NULL )

title: Write the JSON sidecar (no rendering) — write_results_json
text: results Combined results list (e.g. list(attrition = ..., density = ...), optionally data_source = ...). output_dir Directory write report (created needed). cdm_schema CDM schema name (report header / JSON). cohort_id Cohort definition id (report header / JSON). package_version Package version string. Defaults installed rwevalidate version. run_date Timestamp run. Defaults base::Sys.time(). check_summary Optional pre-built summary; rebuilt NULL.

title: Write the JSON sidecar (no rendering) — write_results_json
text: path written JSON file (invisibly).

title: rwevalidate 0.1.0
text: First public release. rwevalidate validates instantiated OMOP CDM cohort real-world-evidence use produces regulator-aligned HTML + JSON report single validate_cohort() call.

title: rwevalidate 0.1.0
text: cdm_connect() / cdm_disconnect() - open PostgreSQL CDM connection validate required clinical tables exist (split-schema aware). Module 1 - run_concepts() - concept prevalence, ancestor coverage, per-domain vocabulary mapping rate (FDA Relevance). Module 2 - run_attrition() - cohort size, index-date distribution, prior-observation coverage, demographics index (HARPER / RECORD-PE). Module 3 - run_density() - records per patient per month domain follow-completeness censoring breakdown (FDA Reliability). Module 4 - run_covariates() - standardized mean differences (love plot), per-arm prevalence, power calculation comparator (HARPER). Reporting - render_validation_report() writes self-contained HTML report traffic-light summary plus machine-readable validation_results.json sidecar; write_results_json() works without Pandoc. validate_cohort() - one entry point wires connection, modules, report. Accepts open con PostgreSQL connection arguments; concept_ids enables Module 1, comparator_id enables Module 4.

title: rwevalidate 0.1.0
text: Targets OMOP CDM v5.3.1. Validated MIMIC-IV PostgreSQL synthetic DuckDB mock used test suite. dependency CohortDiagnostics, DataQualityDashboard, Achilles. thresholds documented, configurable function arguments.
